Defines placeholder functions for I2C rail control and configuration in the Silk platform.

# Purpose
This C source code file includes several header files and defines two functions, [`silk_rail_ctl_fn`](<#silk_rail_ctl_fn>) and [`silk_rail_cfg_fn`](<#silk_rail_cfg_fn>), which are currently not implemented (indicated by the "NYI" comment, meaning "Not Yet Implemented"). The file includes headers for power management IC (PMIC) and GPIO drivers, as well as utility functions for delay and board-specific configurations. Additionally, it includes headers for STM32F4xx series microcontroller peripherals such as GPIO, RCC, and I2C, suggesting that the functions are intended to control or configure power rails on a board using these peripherals. The [`silk_rail_ctl_fn`](<#silk_rail_ctl_fn>) function takes a boolean parameter `enable`, which implies it will control the state of a power rail, while [`silk_rail_cfg_fn`](<#silk_rail_cfg_fn>) is likely intended for configuration purposes.

- `drivers/pmic.h`
- `drivers/gpio.h`

- `board/board.h`
